title: Question 5
stem: Which of the following represents the maximum amount of money that can be collected from all sources (insurance and patient) for a laboratory procedure?
teaching_point: . The contractual amount is the amount that the provider, based on contracted agreement, has agreed to accept for the service provided. It includes the amount that the insurance company will pay and the amount that is to be collected as a copay from the patient. The gross charge, which is also the fee schedule charge, is typically set high to ensure maximum collection from any payer and does not reflect what will ultimately be received. Payment from the insurance payer may reflect a significant portion of the contractual amount; however, the patient must be billed for the difference. Contractual adjustment is the difference between the gross charge and the contractual amount, reflecting effectively a discount off of the gross charge. Revenue cycle management

title: Question 8
stem: Which of the following scenarios is least likely to benefit from a root cause analysis?
teaching_point: Daily monitoring of personnel punctuality to ensure adequacy of shift coverage. RCA is most practical when used as an investigative tool to uncover and address the chief underlying reason(s) for an undesired event, trend or outcome. It has less utility as a proactive surveillance tool. The daily monitoring of personnel punctuality to ensure adequacy of shift coverage is an ongoing surveillance activity; unless a negative trend is discovered, RCA is not likely to be directly applicable. The underlying reason (root cause) for increased turnaround time in surgical pathology, specimen mislabeling or incorrect blood product administration may originate from systemic issues beyond what may be immediately visible and thus these scenarios may benefit from RCAs. Similarly, difficulties in personnel recruitment and retention may be due to reasons other than labor market forces (such as institutional / departmental reputation, conflicting personalities, safety concerns, onboarding problems, human resource conflicts) and as such, may benefit from RCA. Causal analysis

title: Question 18
stem: A 51 year old woman presented at a community clinic with an infected wound on her foot that she reports having for ~1 month. She states that 3 years ago she was seen at the clinic for excessive fatigue and blurry vision and chart consultation indicates that she was diagnosed with diabetes mellitus. At that time, she was given insulin but says that once it ran out, she did not get more. If this patient did not get the medicine because she did not know that she had a chronic condition and thought it would resolve on its own, which Healthy People 2030 social determinant of health could primarily account for this healthcare shortcoming?
teaching_point: Education: access and quality. Health education and the learned ability to understand one's own health status is the likely issue in this scenario. Reported barriers to health education, primarily in lower income communities include limited English proficiency, lack of healthcare related practical skills (e.g., injections), lack of access or understanding of medical information and community misinformation. Any of these barriers could have contributed to this patient's misunderstanding of her disease status. Answers A, D and E are incorrect because they are indirectly causative in this case; however, they are likely partially correct depending on circumstance. access to healthcare was not the primary issue in this scenario; however, low quality healthcare and inadequate information from the provider may have contributed to this case. Community health clinics can be overburdened with patients resulting in staffing shortages or inadequate availability for patient education (e.g., translator, patient educator). It is imperative that providers and clinics ensure that patients understand their health condition and the long term implications of noncompliance. Social determinants of health
